titles.title: idiolect: An R package for forensic authorship analysis

citation.article_title: TXT 4N6: Method, consistency, and distinctiveness in the analysis of SMS text messages
citation.author: Grant
citation.unstructured_citation: Grant, T. (2013). TXT 4N6: Method, consistency, and distinctiveness in the analysis of SMS text messages. Journal of Law and Policy, 21, 467–494.

citation.article_title: A forensic authorship analysis of the Ayia Napa rape statement
citation.author: Donlan
citation.journal_title: Methodologies and Challenges in Forensic Linguistics Casework
citation.unstructured_citation: Donlan, L., & Nini, A. (2022). A forensic authorship analysis of the Ayia Napa rape statement. In I. Picornell, R. Perkins, & M. Coulthard (Eds.), Methodologies and Challenges in Forensic Linguistics Casework (pp. 29–43). Wiley-Blackwell. https://doi.org/10.1002/9781394266661.ch3

citation.author: Grieve
citation.unstructured_citation: Grieve, J., Chiang, E., Clarke, I., Gideon, H., Heini, A., Nini, A., & Waibel, E. (2019). Attributing the Bixby Letter using n-gram tracing. Digital Scholarship in the Humanities, 34(3), 493–512. https://doi.org/10.1093/llc/fqy042

citation.author: Leonard
citation.journal_title: The International Journal of the Humanities

citation.author: Juola

citation.author: Potha
citation.journal_title: Experimental IR Meets Multilinguality, Multimodality, and Interaction

citation.article_title: Grammar as a behavioral biometric: Using cognitively motivated grammar models for authorship verification
citation.author: Nini
citation.unstructured_citation: Nini, A., Halvani, O., Graner, L., Titze, S., Gherardi, V., & Ishihara, S. (forthcoming). Grammar as a behavioral biometric: Using cognitively motivated grammar models for authorship verification. Humanities and Social Sciences Communications. Forthcoming. https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2403.08462
